text: The surgeon performs a liver biopsy to assess fatty liver and other pathologies. A laparoscopic cholecystectomy is conducted, followed by the mobilization of the right liver using ultrasonic shears. The short hepatic vein is dissected and ligated with a Hem-o-lok clip.

The surgeon meticulously dissects and isolates the common bile duct, right hepatic artery, and portal vein using vessel loops. Temporary clamping of the right portal vein and right hepatic artery is performed, followed by marking the demarcation line with electric cautery. The middle hepatic vein branches for segments V and VIII are ligated. After completing the parenchymal transection, the right hepatic duct, portal vein, and hepatic artery are divided.
The surgeon extracts the right liver graft through a suprapubic transverse incision. Fibrin glue is applied to the cut surface of the liver, and a drain is inserted before concluding the operation.
